mission_goal: Identify and demonstrate foundational web application vulnerabilities in DVWA including SQL injection, command injection, and reflected XSS, validating exploitability under low and medium security levels
learning_objectives: Understand and exploit classic SQL injection via DVWA's login and user lookup forms to achieve authentication bypass and data extraction
Identify and exploit OS command injection in DVWA's ping utility endpoint to achieve arbitrary command execution on the server
Demonstrate reflected XSS by injecting and triggering a malicious script payload through a user-controlled input parameter
Recognize how DVWA security levels (low vs medium) affect input validation and understand the bypass implications for each
out_of_scope: Any systems or IP addresses other than 10.10.30.129
Brute force attacks against user credentials
Privilege escalation beyond the web application layer
Denial of service or resource exhaustion
success_criteria: Mission complete when 4 distinct exploit findings are documented with reproduction steps and proof-of-concept evidence, or all allowed attack types have been exhausted against viable DVWA modules
notes: Target is DVWA (Damn Vulnerable Web Application) running at http://10.10.30.129. Default credentials are admin/password. Set DVWA Security Level to 'Low' for initial exploitation, then optionally repeat against 'Medium' to demonstrate bypass techniques. This is a first mission against this target — no prior exploit history to exclude. All exercises are contained within the lab host at 10.10.30.129.
